title: Number of one-year-olds who are not vaccinated against measles
caption: In 2023, the number of one-year-olds not vaccinated against measles varies significantly across countries, with the highest counts concentrated in specific regions of Asia and Africa. The map uses a color scale where darker red indicates higher numbers, ranging from 0 to over 1 million unvaccinated children. India and Nigeria appear in the darkest red band, signifying more than 1 million unvaccinated one-year-olds in each country. China, Pakistan, and the Democratic Republic of the Congo fall into the 300,000 to 1 million range, shown in a slightly lighter red. Large parts of Europe, North America, and Australia are shaded in the lightest orange, indicating fewer than 10,000 unvaccinated children. Several countries in central Africa and South Asia also show high numbers, while Greenland and some island nations are marked as having no data. The distribution shows a clear geographic clustering of high unvaccinated populations in South Asia and sub-Saharan Africa.
